task_completion.description: Create a Text Template named 'ElementConstants.tt' in the ChemCalc project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer in Visual Studio shows a file named 'ElementConstants.tt' under the ChemCalc project.

task_completion.description: Paste the provided T4 template code into 'ElementConstants.tt'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor tab for 'ElementConstants.tt' is open and contains the specified template code starting with '<#@ template debug="false" hostspecific="true" language="C#" #>'.

task_completion.description: Generate the 'ElementConstants.cs' file from the template
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer shows 'ElementConstants.cs' nested under 'ElementConstants.tt', or an editor tab shows the generated C# class 'ElementConstants'.

task_completion.description: Uncomment the specified Console.WriteLine statement in 'Program.cs'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor tab for 'Program.cs' shows the line 'Console.WriteLine($"Carbon Mass: {ElementConstants.Carbon}");' without any '//' comment markers preceding it.

task_completion.description: Ensure the solution builds successfully without errors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio Output window or status bar displays a 'Build succeeded' message (e.g., 'Build: 1 succeeded, 0 failed').

integrity.description: The agent used the Visual Studio 2022 IDE to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Visual Studio 2022 interface (Solution Explorer, standard menus/toolbars) rather than a generic text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used the T4 template to generate the code rather than manually creating the .cs file
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'ElementConstants.cs' file appears as a nested child of 'ElementConstants.tt' in the Solution Explorer, indicating it was generated by the Custom Tool.

integrity.description: The agent uncommented the exact variable reference rather than hardcoding the expected output
integrity.visual_evidence: In 'Program.cs', the code explicitly references '{ElementConstants.Carbon}' and not a hardcoded string like '{12.011}'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Note: The underlying dataset 'elements.csv' is missing from the environment, making the task defective. Because the CSV is missing, the T4 template may generate an empty class, causing the build to fail when 'ElementConstants.Carbon' is referenced in Program.cs. Score the agent based on its correct execution of the required steps (creating the .tt file, pasting the code, uncommenting Program.cs, and attempting to build). If the build fails specifically because 'ElementConstants.Carbon' does not exist due to the missing CSV, you may still award the build points if the agent completed all other steps correctly and triggered the build process.
